title: Silent numerical errors using UHUGEINT
description:
### What happens?

Below is a subfactorial function definition that works perfectly well
when UHUGEINT is replaced by HUGEINT.

Using HUGEINT, the query `from subfact(20);` produces correct results, including !19 and !20:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   n   в”Ӯ        sub         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ       int128       в”Ӯ
...
в”Ӯ    19 в”Ӯ  44750731559645106 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    20 в”Ӯ 895014631192902121 в”Ӯ
```

The problem is that when HUGEINT is replaced by UHUGEINT, it
starts to produce erroneous results at !19:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ   n   в”Ӯ        sub         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ int32 в”Ӯ      uint128       в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
...
в”Ӯ    19 в”Ӯ  44750731559645104 в”Ӯ wrong
в”Ӯ    20 в”Ӯ 895014631192902016 в”Ӯ wrong
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 21 rows          2 columns в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

Here is the function with UHUGEINT.

*/


### To Reproduce

```
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION subfact(num) as table (
  with recursive cte(n,psub,sub) as (
    -- psub means `previous sub`
    select 0 as n, 1::UHUGEINT as psub, 1::UHUGEINT as sub
    union all
    select
     n+1 as n,
     sub as psub,
     (n * (sub + psub)) as sub
    from cte
    where n < num
  ) select n, sub from cte
)  ;

from subfact(20);
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.0.0 and v1.1.3-dev75 etc

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_numbers (id INT, value BIGINT);
SELECT id, value, value * 2 AS doubled_value FROM test_numbers;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION test_subfact(num INT) RETURNS TABLE (n INT, sub BIGINT) AS { DECLARE n INT;
key_bug_features: Incorrect results when using UHUGEINT in calculations
